title: Using microdata for research | Stats NZ
content:
## Using microdata for research
23 August 2022, 12:00pm
Stats NZ has two large integrated research databases of microdata.
The Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) holds microdata about people and households.
The Longitudinal Business Database (LBD) holds microdata about businesses.
Researchers can apply to access microdata for research in the public interest . Approved researchers can work on these projects in the Data Lab, a service that is available at Stats NZ offices and approved research facilities.
How we keep integrated data safe describes the safeguards we have in place around data access.
Apply to use microdata for research is a step-by-step explanation of the application process.
The code modules initiative is a cross-agency effort to make IDI and LDB data easier to use, and more understandable for everyone.
Agencies and organisations can also apply to integrate new data, where data they supply is added and linked to the IDI or LBD.
How to apply to integrate new data describes the process and criteria for doing this.
Page updated 20 December 2023.
